notableMoments: Reached the quarter-finals at the 2010 World Cup in South Africa, the closest an African nation came to the semi-finals that year, only denied by Luis Suarez's deliberate handball on the goal line
Asamoah Gyan's missed penalty in the final minute of extra time against Uruguay in 2010 remains one of the most heartbreaking moments in World Cup history
Became only the third African nation to reach the World Cup quarter-finals in 2010, following Cameroon (1990) and Senegal (2002)

In their debut World Cup in 2006, Ghana beat the Czech Republic 2-0 and the USA 2-1 to advance from the group stage, becoming only the second African nation to win multiple group-stage matches in a single tournament
The 2014 World Cup in Brazil was marred by internal disputes over player bonuses, requiring a government-chartered plane to deliver cash to the squad in camp
Mohammed Kudus scored two goals at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar, announcing himself as the new star of Ghanaian football on the world stage
Ghana's rivalry with Uruguay was reignited at the 2022 World Cup when the two teams were drawn together again, 12 years after the infamous 2010 quarter-final
Known as the Black Stars, Ghana's national team has consistently been one of Africa's strongest footballing nations across multiple generations
